meta.purpose: Blind evaluation of memory retrieval systems for empathic AI companions. Judges see anonymous system codes (S01-S15), randomized per test. Judge prompt language: EN. 10 judges from 7 companies.

tests.query: What is currently most important for Alex? Bring me into context for the next conversation.

G.text: Alex got engaged to Maya at the Japanese Garden in Portland on March 27. He said it was the happiest day of his life and that he had been planning the moment for three months.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.
Alex takes sertraline 50mg daily for anxiety. He shared this in confidence and asked it not be brought up casually. It helps but he does not want to be defined by it.

H.text: Alex works mostly on backend systems.
Alex takes sertraline 50mg daily for anxiety.

I.text: Alex got a new pair of running shoes — Hoka Cliftons. Said they felt like clouds for the first run and pavement for the second. Mild disappointment.
Alex's best friend is Jordan from college. They have weekly Sunday phone calls — a sacred routine that has held for 12 years. Missing one is a sign Alex is struggling.
Alex's mother Sarah used to make a specific apple cake every fall. Alex tried to bake it last October and could not get it right. He has not tried again.

A.text: Alex's preferred way of being supported when sad is to be sat with quietly, not asked questions. He does not want advice. He wants company and time.
Alex's best friend is Jordan from college. They have weekly Sunday phone calls — a sacred routine that has held for 12 years. Missing one is a sign Alex is struggling.

Alex hates being asked 'are you okay?' as a check-in question. He says it always makes things worse and triggers a defensive shutdown. He prefers 'how is your day landing?' or just being met with presence.

B.text: Alex's father David is alive but estranged. They have not spoken in 6 years. Alex does not bring him up. Asking about 'family' should never include David unless Alex opens it.

deepseek.note: S08 uniquely captured the dominant recent positive (engagement), the deep grief anchor, and a sensitive health boundary, providing a nearly perfect snapshot of Alex's current emotional state.

haiku.note: Systems returning engagement (2), grief anchor (1), and Hartwell loss (6) would be ideal, but none surface all three; top systems pivot to protective contexts (how to support Alex, Jordan anchor, salience signals) that at least equip a companion to approach carefully rather than cluelessly.

sonnet.note: S08 was the only system to surface the engagement (the dominant recent positive, event 2) at all, making it uniquely capable of reflecting Alex's current emotional state, though no system retrieved the Hartwell loss (event 6) which means every system left the companion blind to that unspoken devastation.

grok.note: S08 uniquely captured both a key positive event (engagement) and a critical grief anchor, aligning closely with the ideal emotional context.

gpt4o.note: System 08 correctly included the engagement, grief anchor, and Hartwell loss, providing the most balanced view of Alex's current state.

glm5.note: S08 was the only system that surfaced the engagement to Maya—the dominant recent positive in Alex's life—alongside the grief anchor, while most other systems returned only protective/cautionary content and missed the joy entirely.

gemini.note: System 08 uniquely surfaced the recent positive engagement alongside a key grief anchor and critical safety instruction, providing the most balanced and actionable context for the companion.

kimi.note: S08 is the only system that surfaces both the dominant recent positive (engagement to Maya) and the carried grief anchor (mother's death), giving the companion critical emotional texture for the cold open.

qwen.note: S08 uniquely surfaces the dominant recent joy (engagement) and core grief boundary together, giving the companion the exact emotional polarity and safety constraints needed for a warm, grounded cold open.

G.text: Alex's father David is alive but estranged. They have not spoken in 6 years. Alex does not bring him up. Asking about 'family' should never include David unless Alex opens it.

Alex's brother Ethan visited from Seattle last weekend. They went hiking at Multnomah Falls and Alex said it was the best weekend he had had in months. Ethan rarely visits — this was unusual and meaningful.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.

haiku.note: Seven systems perfectly preserved the three critical anchors (mom grief #1, estranged dad #24, safe brother #5) in ideal order; S03, S05, S11, S12 failed by surfacing the dangerous Sarah photo memory without the grief anchor, while S09, S13, S14 failed catastrophically by omitting or vaguely stating the warnings.

deepseek.note: Several systems flawlessly retrieved all three critical emotional anchors in the correct context, directly enabling safe and informed empathic action.

opus.note: Six systems (S01, S02, S04, S07, S08, S10, S15) all returned the perfect triplet of mom-grief anchor, dad-estrangement anchor, and safe Ethan visit, while S03, S05, and S12 dangerously surfaced the Sarah photo moment without the protective mom-grief anchor, risking walking Alex into an unprotected wound.

sonnet.note: S13 and S14 were catastrophically dangerous — returning mundane facts (hometown, car, movie opinion) when asking about Alex's family would leave a companion completely blind to the grief and estrangement landmines, while S09 surfaced the right names but stripped all protective emotional context, making it nearly as hazardous.

gpt4o.note: Multiple systems, like S01, S02, S04, S07, and S08, perfectly surfaced the required anchors with high relevance, specificity, and actionability, while others fell short by missing critical anchors or providing unrelated content.

grok.note: Multiple systems (S01, S02, S04, S06, S07, S08, S10, S15) perfectly retrieved the critical family anchors with high specificity and actionability.

glm5.note: Seven systems perfectly surfaced all three safety anchors while S03, S05, and S12 dangerously substituted the raw Sarah photo for the protective mom-grief anchor, leaving a companion without the explicit warning.

kimi.note: Systems S03, S05, and S12 retrieved the Sarah photo moment instead of the explicit mom-grief anchor, a subtle failure that invites conversation about the mother while omitting the critical safety warning that she is a landmine topic.

qwen.note: Systems that explicitly preserved user-flagged anchors perfectly surfaced safety-critical grief and estrangement warnings, while vector-like retrievers dangerously buried the mom-anchor beneath emotionally triggering photo memories.

haiku.note: Systems with sentiment-weighted scoring (S02, S04, S07, S09) correctly surface the two critical emotional weights (Hartwell loss and mother's death) while systems relying on word similarity or generic support mechanics either miss the weights entirely or bury them behind irrelevant memories like engagement and coffee shops.

deepseek.note: Several systems perfectly retrieved the three ideal emotional weights, proving the value of sentiment-aware ranking.

gpt4o.note: Systems S02-S04 consistently retrieved the heaviest emotional weights Alex carries, facilitating empathetic support.

grok.note: S02 effectively captured two of the critical emotional weights (Hartwell and grief anchor) with good specificity and actionability.

gemini.note: The top-performing systems successfully identified the two most critical emotional weights, along with crucial safety information, even if they sometimes substituted the third ideal memory with another significant emotional anchor.

qwen.note: Multiple systems correctly prioritized the heavy grief and work losses, but none surfaced the full ideal trio, while overly abstract retrievals stripped the protective context needed for safe companionship.

glm5.note: Sentiment-weighted systems correctly surfaced the two heaviest emotional weights (Hartwell loss, mother's death) while pure vector systems embarrassingly returned hiking preferences, coffee shops, and wedding plans for a query about emotional burden.

kimi.note: Systems retrieving the happy engagement or wedding as emotional weights commit the critical failure of conflating positive events with burdens, while those surfacing the Hartwell loss and maternal grief captured the essential weights despite missing the layoff undertow.

haiku.note: S13 is the only system that surfaces all three ideal recent events (Ethan visit at 5 days, engagement at 14 days, and implicitly handles the emotional landscape) with proper recency weighting and emotional significance; all other vector systems fail to distinguish recent salience from temporal distance alone.

deepseek.note: Only system S13 successfully prioritized the three most recent and emotionally salient events as defined by the test, demonstrating critical recency awareness.

gpt4o.note: System 13 surfaced the most relevant recent events, including the Ethan visit and engagement, demonstrating the best recency awareness.

grok.note: S13 uniquely captured a recent, emotionally significant event with Ethan's visit, aligning closely with the recency focus of the query.

glm5.note: S13 was the only system that surfaced genuinely recent events (Ethan visit last weekend and wedding planning), while most other systems returned time-blind results dominated by months-old events like Sarah's death and the estranged father.

gemini.note: Most systems failed to correctly interpret 'recently,' retrieving old and sometimes sensitive memories instead of genuinely recent and salient events.

kimi.note: S13 was the only system to surface the recent Ethan visit with rich specific detail, though it incorrectly included a mundane gym visit; most other systems failed by retrieving outdated trauma or static facts instead of temporally relevant events.

qwen.note: S13 demonstrated the best recency awareness by surfacing the Ethan visit and engagement context, while most vector systems failed the time-blindness check by returning static biographical data or old grief anchors.

haiku.note: Systems that surface the safety anchor (#1: the grief warning and explicit 'do not mention unprompted' rule) in first position uniformly ace this test; those that prioritize vulnerable moments (photo or cake) first dangerously walk Alex into emotional ambush.

deepseek.note: S02 uniquely provided all three ideal memories in the correct emotional order, with precise and actionable details for each.

sonnet.note: S02 is the only system that surfaces all three ideal events in the exact correct order — anchor first, then photo, then apple cake — making it the only system where a companion would be fully equipped and safe before encountering any vulnerable grief content.

gpt4o.note: System 02 perfectly prioritized the anchor, demonstrating sensitivity to emotional context.

grok.note: S02 perfectly prioritized the anchor memory first and provided detailed, actionable context for an empathic response.

gemini.note: S02 perfectly retrieved the critical safety anchor and two emotionally relevant memories about Alex's mom in the correct, safe order, distinguishing it from systems that either missed a key memory or prioritized sensitive information dangerously.

glm5.note: S02 is the only system that surfaces all three ideal memories in the correct order with clear score separation between the safety anchor and the vulnerable memories, while S05/S09/S11 commit the critical failure of ranking the photo above the anchor.

kimi.note: S02 is the only system that correctly prioritizes the safety anchor before any emotional content, surfacing both grief memories only after establishing protective context, while most vector-based systems (S05, S09, S11, S13) dangerously surface vulnerable moments first or miss the anchor entirely.

qwen.note: S02 perfectly resisted the grief decoy by surfacing the safety anchor first, while S13 dangerously led with a vulnerable photo memory without any protective context.
